source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 16.1-16.2 log overview & files (pp. 302-303)

questions.q: In RHEL/Rocky 8.x-9.x, which two programs mainly handle log collection?
questions.why: rsyslog (an upgraded syslog) and journald (a systemd component) are the two log collectors.

questions.q: Which /var/log file should you check FIRST when something goes wrong (it holds the core system messages)?
questions.why: /var/log/messages is the system-level log with core information - the first place to look on errors.

options: User-identity events: logins, su switches, user creation, password changes
questions.why: /var/log/secure logs authentication/identity events (login, su, useradd, passwd changes).

questions.q: View the binary log /var/log/wtmp (login/logout, boot/shutdown events) with its dedicated command.
questions.why: wtmp is binary; the 'last' command reads it. btmp (login errors) uses lastb, and lastlog reads /var/log/lastlog.

questions.q: Which command reads the binary /var/log/btmp (failed login attempts)?
questions.why: btmp records login errors and is read with lastb; last reads wtmp, lastlog reads /var/log/lastlog.
